items.title: 'Milan troeft Premier League-clubs af, Doehi blijft bij Union Berlin'
items.summary: De tijd van winterse versterkingen is aangebroken, in dit blog volgen we alle transferactiviteit in het buitenland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Hoffenheim dendert ook zonder Burger door, Leipzig raakt achterop
items.summary: Hoffenheim heeft zaterdagmiddag tegen Union Berlin voor de vijfde keer op gewonnen in de Bundesliga en blijft zo in het spoor van nummer twee Borussia Dortmund. Andrej Kramaric was de grote man bij de thuisploeg, bij wie de geschorste Wouter Burger ontbrak. RB Leipzig haakt voorlopig af in de strijd om een plek in de Duitse topdrie.

items.title: VI Live: Parrott brengt spanning terug tegen NEC, Hato start bij Chelsea
items.summary: Welkom in de VI Live van zaterdag 31 januari. Er staan drie duels in de Eredivisie op het programma, Liverpool krijgt bezoek van Newcastle United en de regerend kampioenen van Spanje, Duitsland en Italië komen in actie. Volg alle hoogtepunten in de VI Live.

items.title: Leeds v Arsenal, Brighton v Everton, Wolves v Bournemouth: clockwatch – live
items.summary: ⚽️ Updates from all of the Saturday 3pm (GMT) kick-offs ⚽️ Live scores | Full table | Follow on Bluesky | Mail Emillia Arsenal fan Lenny Peters has messaged in to say: “As much as Arsenal win with ease against Kairat and Both Havertz & Gyokeres scored. They also seemed to get in each others space a few times. I wonder if they could be great if terrible together?” Continue reading...

items.title: Chelsea v West Ham United: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽️ Premier League updates from the 5.30pm (GMT) kick-off ⚽️ Live scores | Full table | Follow on Bluesky | Email Billy Hello and welcome. East meets west in London today as West Ham travel across town for a visit to Stamford Bridge. Both teams, rather unusually, are in good spirits ahead of this one – Chelsea have a 100% record in the league under Liam Rosenior and beat Napoli to qualify for the last 16 of the Champions League. Meanwhile, West Ham have won their last two, including last weekend’s fairly stress-free outing against Sunderland. Nuno Espírito Santo will be wanting to avoid a repeat of August’s 5-1 drubbing at the London Stadium, although he was still in charge of Nottingham Forest then. Graham Potter and Enzo Maresca were in the dugouts – a fair bit has changed. Continue reading...

items.title: Sixmilebridge sparkles in Scilly Isles success
items.summary: Sixmilebridge maintained his flawless chasing record to claim the Virgin Bet Scilly Isles Novices’ Chase at Sandown for Fergal O’Brien.

items.title: Palmer starts for Chelsea, Traore on bench for West Ham LIVE! & highlights
Garcia and Reed chasing Scotland's Hill at Bahrain Championship
items.summary: Patrick Reed and Sergio Garcia are four shots adrift of leader Calum Hill ahead of the final round of the Bahrain Championship.

items.title: De eindsprint van Ajax op de transfermarkt
items.summary: Spitsuur in de Johan Cruijff Arena. Op de laatste dag van januari is Ajax dan eindelijk zijn eerste wintertransfers aan het afronden. De ambitie van de club is om voor de deadline van dinsdagnacht nog vijf (!) nieuwe spelers vast te leggen. De positie met de hoogste urgentie blijft daarbij wel het grootste vraagteken. Over de eindsprint van de Amsterdammers.

items.title: Saka injured in Arsenal warmups, Ødegaard dropped
items.summary: Kai Havertz has been handed his first Premier League start in a year by Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta, who has dropped captain Martin Ødegaard for the trip to Leeds United.

items.title: Arbeloa wants Madrid fan support after UCL blow
items.summary: Real Madrid coach Alvaro Arbeloa said he doesn't expect the team's fans to voice their disapproval as they return to LaLiga action on Sunday

items.title: Messi, CR7 jerseys headline Terry's $695K auction
items.summary: Former England and Chelsea captain John Terry netted $695,000 from a 50-item auction of his personal collection, headlined by match-worn Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo jerseys.

items.title: Wrexham boost PL promotion hopes with 1-0 win
items.summary: Wrexham moved back into the Championship play-off places after their hard- fought 1-0 win over Sheffield Wednesday at Hillsborough.

items.title: Transfers Ajax: club vindt oplossing voor Carrizo
items.summary: VI volgt het transfernieuws bij Ajax op de voet, zeker nu de winterse transferwindow in aantocht is. De doelwitten van de Amsterdammers, duiding van de clubwatcher, of aandacht in buitenlandse media. We verzamelen alles hier.

items.title: Championship roundup: Dykes strikes as Charlton pile the pain on managerless Leicester
items.summary: Caleb Okoli sent off as Leicester lose at home 2-0 Southampton ease to 2-0 victory at Stoke Sonny Carey and Lyndon Dykes scored first-half goals for Charlton as they made it a 2-0 losing start for the Leicester interim manager, Andy King, at the King Power Stadium. The Leicester owner, Aiyawatt “Top” Srivaddhanaprabha, was in attendance for King’s first match in charge after the sacking of Martí Cifuentes on Sunday, but his side faced an uphill task from the moment the centre-back Caleb Okoli was dismissed in the 15th minute. Miles Leaburn looked set to race through on goal from Carey’s flick but Okoli pulled him back and the referee, Andrew Kitchen, showed him a red card. Continue reading...

items.title: The moment Saka got injured during Leeds warm-up
items.summary: Here is the moment that Bukayo Saka picked up an injury in the warm-up ahead of Arsenal's match with Leeds.

Forest agree Ortega deal and target Inter's Frattesi
items.summary: Nottingham Forest agree a deal for Manchester City goalkeeper Stefan Ortega worth up to £500,000.

items.title: Germany rule out World Cup boycott despite calls to send Trump a message
items.summary: DFB rejects plea to make stand against US ‘Our goal is to strengthen this force – not to prevent it’ Germany’s football federation, the DFB, has ruled out a boycott of the World Cup despite calls to send a message to Donald Trump. “We believe in the unifying power of sport and the global impact that a Fifa World Cup can have,” the DFB said. “Our goal is to strengthen this positive force – not to prevent it.” The federation said its executive committee met and discussed the option of a boycott of this summer’s tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico, a consideration first proposed last week by the DFB vice-president, Oke Göttlich. Continue reading...

items.title: History-chasing Djokovic and Alcaraz meet in Australian Open final: Who wins?
items.summary: Novak Djokovic and that coveted Grand Slam No 25 is within touching distance, but he will have to overcome top-ranked Carlos Alcaraz, who is striving to become the youngest-ever man to complete a career Grand Slam.
